admin.title: Administration
admin.subtitle: Manage users, run jobs, and configure system settings

dashboard.step1: Configure your media server connection in Settings → System
dashboard.step2: Select which libraries to sync in Settings → Libraries
dashboard.step3: Run the "Sync Movies" job in Jobs to import your movie library
dashboard.step4: Enable users who should receive AI recommendations in Users
dashboard.step5: Run the "Generate Embeddings" job to create movie embeddings
dashboard.step6: Run the "Generate Recommendations" job to create personalized picks

gaps.pageSubtitle: Compare TMDB movie collections against your synced library to find missing titles. Requests via Seerr are never automatic — you choose what to request.
gaps.statusTmdbBad: Add your TMDb API key in Admin → Settings
gaps.statusCollectionsBad: Run Enrich Metadata (Admin → Jobs) so movies get collection_id from TMDb
gaps.statusSeerrBad: Configure Seerr in Admin → Settings to request missing titles
gaps.inProgressScan: In progress — {{scanned}} collections scanned, {{missing}} missing released titles so far, ~{{pct}}% released parts in library.
gaps.lastRun: Last run: {{when}} — {{scanned}} collections, {{missing}} missing released titles, ~{{pct}}% released parts in library. Re-run after syncing new movies.
gaps.noRunYet: No run yet. Start an analysis to scan your library against TMDb collections.
gaps.scanningCollections: Scanning collections — results appear below as each batch finishes.
gaps.missingSoFar: {{count}} missing released title so far — the grid updates as analysis runs.
gaps.missingSoFar_plural: {{count}} missing released titles so far — the grid updates as analysis runs.
gaps.noMissingInSnapshot: No missing titles in this snapshot (or nothing matches the filter).
gaps.dialogBulkBody: This will send {{count}} request(s) to Seerr using your linked account (Radarr options already chosen). You can track them under My Requests.

usersPage.errorLoadUsers: Failed to load users from media server
usersPage.aiOverrideOn: {{name}} can now control their AI explanation preference
usersPage.aiOverrideOff: {{name}} will use the global AI explanation setting
usersPage.discoverOff: {{name}} will no longer see discovery suggestions
usersPage.discoverReqOff: {{name}} can no longer request discovered content
usersPage.subtitleDesktop: Manage {{provider}} users and enable AI recommendations
usersPage.syncTooltip: Import new users and sync profile data (email, admin status) from media server
usersPage.syncTooltipShort: Sync new users from media server
usersPage.discoverColTooltip: Show discovery suggestions for content not in library
usersPage.requestColTooltip: Allow user to request discovered content via Seerr
usersPage.aiOverrideColTooltip: Allow user to control their AI explanation preference

userDetail.recsEmpty: No recommendations generated yet. Run the recommendations job to generate picks for this user.
userDetail.diagnosticsPlaceholder: Recommendation diagnostics and score breakdowns will appear here.
userDetail.seerrIdInvalid: Seerr user id must be a positive integer or empty to clear
userDetail.watchHistoryBody: Allow this user to mark movies and series as unwatched. This will update both the media server (Emby/Jellyfin) and Aperture's database.
userDetail.watchHistoryToggleHint: User can mark items as unwatched from their Watch History page and movie detail pages
userDetail.discoveryBody: Control this user's access to the Discovery feature, which suggests content not currently in your library based on their taste profile.
userDetail.discoverySuggestionsHint: User can view AI-powered suggestions for movies and series not in the library
userDetail.discoveryRequestHint: User can request discovered content to be added to the library via Seerr
userDetail.discoveryRequiresDiscover: Request permission requires Discovery to be enabled first.
userDetail.seerrMappingBody: Optional override: numeric user id from Seerr (Users in Seerr). When set, content requests are attributed to this Seerr account. Leave empty to rely on automatic matching by email or username.
userDetail.aiExplanationBody: Control whether this user can customize their AI explanation preferences.
userDetail.globalSettingEnabledAllowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are enabled by default (per-user overrides allowed)
userDetail.globalSettingEnabledDisallowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are enabled by default (per-user overrides disabled globally)
userDetail.globalSettingDisabledAllowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are disabled by default (per-user overrides allowed)
userDetail.globalSettingDisabledDisallowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are disabled by default (per-user overrides disabled globally)
userDetail.allowOverrideHint: When enabled, this user can toggle AI explanations on/off for their own recommendations
userDetail.overridesDisabledGlobally: Per-user overrides are disabled globally. Enable "Allow Per-User Overrides" in Settings → AI Config → Algorithm to grant users override permission.
userDetail.userChoseEnable: (User has chosen to enable AI explanations)
userDetail.userChoseDisable: (User has chosen to disable AI explanations)
userDetail.watchHistoryTabEmpty: No watch history found. Run the watch history sync job to import data from the media server.

jobsPage.title: Background Jobs
jobsPage.subtitle: Manage syncing, AI processing, and system maintenance tasks
jobsPage.scheduleSubtitle: Configure when each job runs automatically. Disabled jobs will only run when manually triggered.
jobsPage.enrichmentBody1: A previous enrichment run was interrupted (possibly due to a container restart).
jobsPage.enrichmentRemaining: {{movies}} movies and {{series}} series still need enrichment.
jobsPage.enrichmentCompletedBefore: ({{movies}} movies and {{series}} series were completed before interruption.)
jobsPage.enrichmentFooter: Click Resume to continue enrichment, or Dismiss to clear this warning.

ui.cancelBody: Are you sure you want to stop {{name}}? The job will be cancelled and any progress will be lost.
ui.historyEmpty: No run history found for this job.
ui.configManualOnlyBody: This job deletes all existing data before rebuilding. It cannot be scheduled and must be run manually. Use only after major algorithm changes, embedding model changes, or if recommendations appear corrupted.
ui.configPreviewManualOnly: Manual only - this job cannot be scheduled

home.heroSubtitle: Here's what we've picked for you based on your watch history

home.topPicksEmpty: No recommendations yet. Your personalized picks will appear here once they're generated.

insights.subtitleSeries: How similar this series is to series you've enjoyed
insights.tooltipDiscovery: How different this is from what you usually watch - helps you discover new things
insights.tooltipVariety: How much variety this adds to your recommendations

insights.basedOnHistoryMovie: Based on your history with similar movies:
insights.basedOnHistorySeries: Based on your history with similar series:

similar.graphHint: Click poster to explore • Click ⓘ for details

imageUpload.dropLabel: Drag & drop an image here, or click to browse
imageUpload.tooltipDefaultBadge: This is the default image set by admin

aiFunctionCard.toolCallingWarningTitle: This model doesn't support reliable tool calling.
aiFunctionCard.toolCallingWarningBody: The assistant will work but cannot search your library or make recommendations.
aiFunctionCard.toolCallingWarningOllama: For Ollama, use firefunction-v2 or qwen3 instead.
aiFunctionCard.embeddingUnsupported: This model doesn't support embeddings. Choose a different model.
aiFunctionCard.ollamaInstallTitle: Install models on your Ollama server
aiFunctionCard.baseUrlHelperOllama: Make sure Ollama is running and accessible at this URL
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_ollama: Enter the exact model name as it appears on your Ollama server. You can find model names at ollama.com/library.
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_openrouter: Enter the exact model name as it appears on your OpenRouter account. You can find model names at openrouter.ai/models (e.g., anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet, openai/gpt-4o).
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_huggingface: Enter the exact model name as it appears on Hugging Face Hub. You can find model names at huggingface.co/models (e.g., meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3-0324).
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_compatible: Enter the exact model name as it appears on your OpenAI-compatible server.
aiFunctionCard.embeddingVectorTitle: Vector Dimension Must Match Model Output
aiFunctionCard.embeddingVectorBody: The embedding dimension you select must exactly match the number of dimensions your model outputs. If these don't match, embedding generation will fail or produce incorrect results.
aiFunctionCard.embeddingCommonDimensionsList:
384 — granite-embedding-30m-english, all-MiniLM-L6-v2
768 — nomic-embed-text, snowflake-arctic-embed-m, e5-base-v2
1024 — mxbai-embed-large, snowflake-arctic-embed-l, voyage-multilingual-2
1536 — OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-ada-002
3072 — OpenAI text-embedding-3-large
4096 — nv-embed-v2, larger custom models
aiFunctionCard.embeddingCheckDocs: Check your model's documentation to find the correct dimension.
aiFunctionCard.validatingModel: Validating model… This may take a moment{{ollamaSuffix}}

chat.notConfiguredMessage: Configure an AI provider for the Chat function to use the assistant.
chat.limitedToolsMessage: Your chat model ({{model}}) doesn't support tool calling. The assistant will work as a basic chatbot without access to your library, search, or recommendations.

recommendations.embeddingsMissingMessage: Configure an embedding provider to enable AI-powered recommendations.
recommendations.textGenMissingMessage: Configure a text generation provider to enable recommendation explanations.

search.semanticUnavailableMessage: Configure an embedding provider to enable semantic search (searching by concept/theme).

general.notConfiguredMessage: Configure AI providers in Settings to enable recommendations, chat, and other AI features.

fullyConfigured.title: AI Fully Configured
fullyConfigured.message: All AI features are available with your current configuration.

topPicksOutputConfig.accordionTitle: What's the difference between Library, Collection, and Playlist?
topPicksOutputConfig.libraryBody: Appears as a separate library in your media server sidebar. Contains virtual copies (STRM files) of the media. Best for dedicated browsing of Top Picks.
topPicksOutputConfig.collectionBody: Groups your existing library items into a "box set" that appears when browsing. Uses your original media files directly. Best for organization within existing libraries.
topPicksOutputConfig.playlistBody: Creates an ordered playlist in the Playlists section. Uses your original media files directly. Best for sequential watching or shuffle play.

assistantToolUi.carouselRecommendationsEmpty: No recommendations generated yet. Ask an admin to run the recommendation job.
assistantToolUi.carouselWatchHistoryDesc: Found {{count}} items in your watch history
assistantToolUi.carouselSearchNoResults: No results found. Try adjusting your filters.

userSettings.profileSyncedCaption: Profile information above is synced from your media server.
userSettings.emailHelperCustom: You have set a custom email (not synced from Emby)
userSettings.emailHelperSynced: Synced from Emby Connect. Edit to override.
userSettings.emailNotificationsSubtitle: Receive email notifications about your recommendations
userSettings.aiLibraryNamesSubtitle: Customize how your AI recommendations libraries appear in your media server.
userSettings.libraryHelperNamedMovies: Your movies library will be named: "{{name}}"
userSettings.libraryHelperNamedSeries: Your series library will be named: "{{name}}"
userSettings.libraryHelperEmpty: Leave empty to use the global default template
userSettings.aiLibraryFooter: Changes will apply the next time the library sync jobs run or when recommendations are regenerated.
userSettings.aiExplanationSubtitle: Choose whether to include AI-generated explanations in your recommendation descriptions.
userSettings.aiExplanationIncludeBody: When enabled, each recommendation includes "Why Aperture picked this for you"
userSettings.aiExplanationFooter: Changes will apply when your recommendations are next regenerated.
userSettings.watchedRecsSubtitle: Choose whether to include content you've already watched in your AI recommendations.
userSettings.includeWatchedDesc: Recommendations may include movies and series you've already watched
userSettings.newContentOnlyDesc: Recommendations will only show content you haven't watched yet
userSettings.watchedRecsFooter: By default, recommendations only include new content. Enable this if you want rewatching suggestions.
userSettings.dislikedSubtitle: Choose how content you've rated 1-3 hearts should be handled in recommendations.
userSettings.excludeDislikedDesc: Content you dislike will never appear in recommendations
userSettings.penalizeDislikedDesc: Content you dislike will appear less often but may still show up occasionally
userSettings.dislikedFooter: Rate content with 1-3 hearts to mark it as disliked. Changes will apply when your recommendations are next regenerated.
userSettings.similarityGraphPrefsSubtitle: Customize how the similarity graph explores connections between movies and series.
userSettings.hideWatchedOn: Graph only shows unwatched content for new discoveries
userSettings.hideWatchedOff: Graph includes all similar content, even what you've already seen
userSettings.fullFranchiseOn: Show entire franchises without limits (e.g., all 26 James Bond films)
userSettings.fullFranchiseOff: Limit items per franchise to encourage diverse discoveries
userSettings.similarityGraphFooter: These preferences apply when exploring similar content on detail pages and in fullscreen graph view.
userSettings.traktSubtitle: Connect your Trakt account to sync your movie and TV show ratings.
userSettings.traktFooter: Syncing imports your Trakt ratings (1-10) as heart ratings in Aperture. Higher-rated content will have more influence on your recommendations.
userSettings.libraryNamesSaved: Library names saved! They will be used for future library updates.

watcherIdentity.successMergeNew: Found {{franchiseCount}} new franchises and {{genreCount}} new genres!
watcherIdentity.successMergeNone: No new items found. Your preferences are up to date!
watcherIdentity.successResetComplete: Analysis complete! Found {{franchiseCount}} franchises and {{genreCount}} genres.
watcherIdentity.analyzeModalBody: You have existing preferences. How would you like to proceed?

watcherIdentity.analyzeMergeDescription: Keep your customized preferences and only add newly detected items

watcherIdentity.minFranchiseSizeCaptionShows: Only include franchises with at least {{count}} shows available
watcherIdentity.minWatchedCaption: You must have watched at least {{count}} from a franchise to include it
watcherIdentity.specificInterestsBody: Add themes, styles, or content types you enjoy to improve recommendations.
watcherIdentity.interestPlaceholder: Type an interest and press Enter

watcherIdentity.librarySourcesDescMovie: Choose which movie libraries contribute to your taste profile.
watcherIdentity.librarySourcesDescTv: Choose which TV show libraries contribute to your taste profile.
watcherIdentity.aiPicksExcludedCaption: AI Picks and system libraries are automatically excluded.
watcherIdentity.franchiseExplainerLead: Adjust how much each franchise influences your recommendations.
watcherIdentity.franchiseExplainerAfterBoost: to see more from franchises you love,
watcherIdentity.franchiseExplainerAfterAvoid: to see less. Your settings are saved automatically.
watcherIdentity.genreExplainerLead: Fine-tune genre preferences to personalize discovery and recommendations.
watcherIdentity.genreExplainerAfterHide: genres you want to avoid. Changes apply to all features.
watcherIdentity.noFranchisesHint: No franchises detected. Click "{{action}}" to scan.
watcherIdentity.noGenresHint: No genres detected. Click "{{action}}" to scan.
watcherIdentity.identitySubtitle: AI-generated analysis of your viewing preferences
watcherIdentity.generateIdentityHintMovie: Click "{{action}}" to create your personalized movie profile
watcherIdentity.generateIdentityHintSeries: Click "{{action}}" to create your personalized TV profile

algorithmSettings.weightSimilarityDesc: How much to prioritize content similar to what you've enjoyed
algorithmSettings.weightNoveltyDesc: How much to prioritize exploring new genres and styles
algorithmSettings.weightRatingDesc: How much to favor highly-rated content
algorithmSettings.weightDiversityDesc: How much variety to include in recommendations
algorithmSettings.masterSubtitle: Fine-tune how Aperture generates recommendations for you. When disabled, admin-configured defaults are used.
algorithmSettings.descCustomActive: Your personalized algorithm settings are active
algorithmSettings.weightTipNormal: Set relative importance for each factor. Weights are automatically normalized.
algorithmSettings.recentWatchTooltip: How many recently watched items to consider when building your taste profile
algorithmSettings.autoSaveFooter: Changes are saved automatically and will take effect when your recommendations are next regenerated.
algorithmSettings.dislikedHandlingSubtitle: How should content you've rated 1-3 hearts affect your recommendations?
algorithmSettings.excludeOptionDesc: Content you dislike will never appear in recommendations
algorithmSettings.penalizeOptionDesc: May still appear if strongly matching other preferences
algorithmSettings.noDislikedItems: No disliked content. Rate content with 1-3 hearts to add items here.
algorithmSettings.dislikedTipFooter: Tip: Adjust hearts to change rating. Click ✕ to clear rating entirely (returns item to unrated pool).

settingsDatabase.title: Database Management
settingsDatabase.dangerBody: These actions are irreversible and will delete data.
settingsDatabase.purgeDescription: Purge all content data to start fresh. This will delete all movies, series, episodes, embeddings, watch history, ratings, recommendations, taste profiles, and assistant conversations. Library configuration and user accounts are preserved.
settingsDatabase.irreversibleBody: You are about to permanently delete ALL content from your database. This includes:

settingsApiKeys.title: API Keys
settingsApiKeys.subtitle: API keys allow programmatic access to the Aperture API. Keys are shown only once when created.
settingsApiKeys.empty: No API keys found. Create your first API key to enable programmatic access.
settingsApiKeys.keyNameHelper: A descriptive name to identify this key
settingsApiKeys.createdWarningBody: Copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again!
settingsApiKeys.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke the API key “{{name}}”?
settingsApiKeys.revokeWarning: This action cannot be undone. Any applications using this key will lose access.

settingsBackup.title: Database Backup & Restore
settingsBackup.backupsToRetainHelper: Number of backup files to keep (older backups are automatically deleted)
settingsBackup.scheduleNote: Automatic backups run daily at 2:00 AM
settingsBackup.noBackups: No backups found. Create your first backup using the button above.
settingsBackup.restoreWarningBody: This will replace all current data with the backup. A pre-restore backup will be created automatically.
settingsBackup.restoreFrom: You are about to restore from: {{filename}}
settingsBackup.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please refresh and try again.

settingsTmdb.title: TMDb Integration
settingsTmdb.description: Enable TMDb integration for keywords, collections/franchises, and expanded crew data.
settingsTmdb.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.

settingsTrakt.registerSuffix: to get your Client ID and Secret.
settingsTrakt.helperSecretSaved: Client secret is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsTrakt.redirectHelper: Copy this URL to your Trakt app's Redirect URI field

settingsOmdb.title: OMDb Integration
settingsOmdb.description: Enable OMDb integration for Rotten Tomatoes scores, Metacritic ratings, and awards data.
settingsOmdb.descriptionSuffix: (1,000 requests/day free, or 100,000/day for paid subscribers).
settingsOmdb.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsOmdb.paidCaptionOff: Enable if you have a paid OMDb key for faster enrichment

settingsMdblist.title: MDBList Integration
settingsMdblist.description: Enable MDBList integration for internet popularity rankings in Top Picks, plus Letterboxd ratings, streaming availability, and additional keywords for metadata enrichment.
settingsMdblist.descriptionSuffix: (1,000 requests/day free, or higher limits for supporters).
settingsMdblist.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsMdblist.supporterCaptionOn: Using faster rate limits for supporters
settingsMdblist.supporterCaptionOff: Enable if you are an MDBList supporter for faster enrichment

settingsSeerr.title: Seerr Integration
settingsSeerr.description: Enable Seerr integration to let users request missing content discovered by AI.
settingsSeerr.learnMoreSuffix: or get your API key from your Seerr settings.
settingsSeerr.urlHelper: The URL of your Seerr instance
settingsSeerr.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsSeerr.helperNewKey: Get your API key from Seerr → Settings → General

settingsStreamingDiscovery.title: Streaming discovery (JustWatch)
settingsStreamingDiscovery.description: Show popular and per-provider streaming charts on the Discovery page. Uses JustWatch public data; enable only after you are comfortable with the integration.
settingsStreamingDiscovery.providerStripsHelper: Pick one or more streaming services. Stored values are short_name codes used by JustWatch filters. For regions outside the bundled list, cross-check with the live Discovery providers response.
settingsStreamingDiscovery.providerTermsLoadWarning: Could not load provider choices for the picker; save still works if you already have codes configured. Refresh the page to retry.
settingsStreamingDiscovery.loadError: Failed to load streaming discovery settings
settingsStreamingDiscovery.saveError: Failed to save streaming discovery settings

settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.title: Discovery — genre strips (TMDb)
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.description: Each block below is one horizontal strip on Discovery. Up to 24 strips per section; up to 8 genres per strip. Genre names follow your UI locale.
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.movieSectionHint: Pick TMDb movie genres for each strip. Several genres in one strip are combined with AND. Exclude genres uses TMDb without_genres (for example Science Fiction but not Animation). Optional start/end years map to Discover release date bounds. Max titles is capped 1–48 (actual results can be lower after library filters). Optional heading overrides the default genre-based title; optional country filters by production origin (TMDb with_origin_country).
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.tvSectionHint: Pick TMDb TV genres for each strip. Several genres in one strip are combined with AND. Exclude genres and year bounds work like movies (first air date for TV). Max titles is capped 1–48. Optional heading and country work the same as for movies.
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.fieldExcludeGenresHelper: TMDb without_genres: titles matching these genres are dropped (for example exclude Animation from Sci-Fi).
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.footerNote: Reordering strips saves automatically. After changing genres, exclude genres, years, limits, heading, or origin, use the save icon on that strip (it saves the whole Movies or TV list for that section). Max titles uses the same 1–48 cap as the Discovery API; fewer cards may show if titles are filtered out.
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.rowCustomTitlePlaceholder: Leave blank to use genre names on Discovery
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.rowCustomTitleHelper: If set, this text is shown as the strip heading instead of joined TMDb genre names (max 80 characters).
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.rowOriginCountryHelper: Optional. Restricts TMDb Discover to titles whose origin country matches (ISO code). Movies and TV both use TMDb’s with_origin_country filter.
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.countriesLoadWarning: Could not load the country list for the picker; you can still save ISO codes if already configured. Refresh the page to retry.
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.rowHelperMovie: TMDb movie genres. Multiple genres in one row are combined (AND).
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.rowHelperTv: TMDb TV genres. Multiple genres in one row are combined (AND).
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.genresLoadWarning: Could not load genre names for the picker; you can still save existing configuration. Refresh the page to retry.
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.loadError: Failed to load genre strip settings
settingsDiscoveryGenreStrips.saveError: Failed to save genre strip settings

settingsMediaServer.title: Media Server
settingsMediaServer.serverUrlHelper: The base URL of your media server
settingsMediaServer.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new key to update it.
settingsMediaServer.helperNew: Generate an API key in your media server admin settings
settingsMediaServer.fillFieldsBeforeTest: Please fill in all fields before testing
settingsMediaServer.helpDocker: Your media server must be accessible from this container. For Docker deployments, use the container name (for example {{exampleUrl}}) or the host IP address.
settingsMediaServer.allowPasswordlessHelper: Enable this if your media server users don't have passwords set
settingsMediaServer.securityWarningBody: Only enable this if your Aperture instance is on a private network with no internet exposure. If your server is accessible via reverse proxy or the internet, leave this disabled to prevent unauthorized access.

settingsFileLocations.title: File Locations
settingsFileLocations.subtitle: Configure where your media server sees Aperture's libraries and media files
settingsFileLocations.detectCaption: Automatically detect paths by comparing media server files with Aperture's mounts
settingsFileLocations.apertureLibrariesHelper: Where your media server sees Aperture's output (the folder mounted as /aperture-libraries in Aperture)
settingsFileLocations.mediaServerPathPrefixHelper: Base path where your media server sees your media files (used for symlinks)
settingsFileLocations.detectSuccess: Paths detected! Click "Save Changes" to apply.

settingsLibraryConfig.title: Library Configuration
settingsLibraryConfig.subtitle: Select which libraries to include when syncing content
settingsLibraryConfig.syncTooltip: Refresh library list from media server
settingsLibraryConfig.noLibrariesMovie: No movie libraries found. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.
settingsLibraryConfig.noLibrariesTv: No TV show libraries found. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.
settingsLibraryConfig.warningNoneEnabled: ⚠️ No libraries enabled — {{type}} sync will be skipped
settingsLibraryConfig.footerNextSync: Changes take effect on the next {{type}} sync. Run the appropriate sync job after making changes.
settingsLibraryConfig.emptyState: No libraries configured yet. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.

settingsOutputFormat.title: AI Recommendations Output Format
settingsOutputFormat.subtitle: Choose how AI recommendation files are created for the media server
settingsOutputFormat.loadFailed: Failed to load output format configuration
settingsOutputFormat.moviesSymlinkHelp: Creates symbolic links to original movie files
settingsOutputFormat.moviesStrmHelp: Creates .strm files with paths or streaming URLs
settingsOutputFormat.seriesSymlinkHelp: Creates symlinks to original season folders (recommended)
settingsOutputFormat.seriesStrmRecommendation: Symlinks are recommended for TV series for better performance
settingsOutputFormat.explainSymlinksIntro: Creates symbolic links pointing to your original media files. Faster playback, same quality.
settingsOutputFormat.explainSymlinksRequires: Both Aperture and your media server must access the same filesystem paths.
settingsOutputFormat.explainStrmIntro: Creates .strm files containing paths or streaming URLs. Works in all network configurations.
settingsOutputFormat.explainStrmBestFor: Docker setups, different machines, or when paths differ between systems.
settingsOutputFormat.symlinkWarningBody: Symlinks require that Aperture can access your media files at the exact same paths that your media server uses. If you're running Aperture in Docker, ensure the volume mounts match.

settingsMdblistSelector.manualPlaceholder: Enter list ID or paste MDBList URL…
settingsMdblistSelector.manualInfoSuffix: and paste the URL or ID here.
settingsMdblistSelector.emptyMyLists: You don't have any lists on MDBList
settingsMdblistSelector.emptySearchShort: Type at least 2 characters to search
settingsMdblistSelector.errInvalidId: Please enter a valid list ID or MDBList URL

settingsAiSetup.pageSubtitle: Configure AI providers for different features. Aperture recommends using OpenAI for all four functions, but feel free to explore other providers and models.
settingsAiSetup.cardEmbeddingsDesc: Converts text to vectors for semantic search and recommendations. Higher dimensions = better quality.
settingsAiSetup.cardChatDesc: Powers the AI assistant. Needs tool calling to search your library and make recommendations.
settingsAiSetup.cardTextGenDesc: Generates recommendation explanations, taste profiles, and playlist descriptions.
settingsAiSetup.cardExplorationDesc: Powers the Explore page. Uses AI to find meaningful connections from conceptual searches like "feel-good comedies".
settingsAiSetup.embeddingSetsBody: You have embeddings stored from multiple models. Inactive sets can be deleted to free up storage. When you switch back to a model with existing embeddings, they will be used immediately without regenerating.
settingsAiSetup.confirmDeleteSet: Delete all embeddings for "{{model}}"? This cannot be undone.

settingsAiExplanation.loadFailed: Failed to load AI explanation configuration
settingsAiExplanation.title: AI Explanation in Recommendations
settingsAiExplanation.subtitle: Control whether AI-generated explanations ("Why Aperture picked this for you") appear in recommendation descriptions
settingsAiExplanation.includeDesc: When enabled, each recommendation includes a personalized explanation of why it was selected
settingsAiExplanation.overrideDesc: When enabled, administrators can grant individual users the ability to toggle AI explanations for their own recommendations
settingsAiExplanation.infoThreeTierBody: Global default → Admin grants override per user → User chooses their preference.
settingsAiExplanation.infoGrantPath: To grant a user override permission, go to Users → select a user → enable "Allow AI explanation override".

settingsCostEstimator.configureFirst: Configure your AI providers above to see cost estimates.
settingsCostEstimator.title: Cost Estimator
settingsCostEstimator.tooltip: Estimates based on your configured providers. Actual costs may vary.
settingsCostEstimator.localNoteBody: Running AI locally means $0.00 API costs for those functions. Only your compute costs (electricity, hardware) apply.
settingsCostEstimator.initialCaption: One-time cost to embed your entire library ({{movies}} movies, {{series}} series, {{episodes}} episodes)
settingsCostEstimator.recurringCaption: Ongoing costs for recommendations, explanations, and chat

settingsRecAlgo.title: Recommendation Algorithm
settingsRecAlgo.subtitle: Configure how recommendations are generated. Hover the help icons next to each setting for detailed explanations.
settingsRecAlgo.loadFailed: Could not load recommendation configuration. Try refreshing the page.
settingsRecAlgo.footer: Changes apply to the next recommendation generation run.

maxCandidates.title: Max Candidates
maxCandidates.description: How many items from your library to evaluate as potential recommendations.
maxCandidates.increase: More thorough search, considers entire library, but slower processing.
maxCandidates.decrease: Faster generation, but may miss some good matches from less popular items.
maxCandidates.example: Set to ∞ (unlimited) for best results if you have time, or 10-20K for faster daily runs.

selectedCount.title: Recommendations Per User
selectedCount.description: The final number of recommendations each user receives.
selectedCount.increase: More choices for users, but quality of later picks may decrease.
selectedCount.decrease: Fewer but higher-quality, more confident recommendations.
selectedCount.example: Movies: 20-50 is typical. Series: 10-15 since they require more time investment.

recentWatchLimit.title: Watch History Depth
recentWatchLimit.description: How many recently watched items to analyze when building the taste profile.
recentWatchLimit.increase: Broader understanding of long-term preferences, better for established users.
recentWatchLimit.decrease: Focuses on recent viewing habits, good if tastes change frequently.
recentWatchLimit.example: New user with 20 watches? Set to 20. Long-time user? 50-100 captures their full taste.

similarityWeight.title: Taste Similarity
similarityWeight.description: How much to favor items that match the user's established preferences.
similarityWeight.increase: Safer picks the user will likely enjoy, but less adventurous.
similarityWeight.decrease: More variety and surprises, but higher chance of misses.
similarityWeight.example: Set high (50-60%) for picky viewers. Set lower (30-40%) for those who like discovering new things.

noveltyWeight.title: Genre Discovery
noveltyWeight.description: Rewards items with genres the user hasn't explored much.
noveltyWeight.increase: Pushes users outside their comfort zone into new genres.
noveltyWeight.decrease: Sticks to familiar territory and known favorite genres.
noveltyWeight.example: Someone who only watches action? Higher novelty might suggest a thriller or sci-fi they'd enjoy.

ratingWeight.title: Community Rating
ratingWeight.description: How much to favor highly-rated, critically acclaimed items.
ratingWeight.increase: Prioritizes quality over personalization — great for new users.
ratingWeight.decrease: Ignores ratings, trusts the taste-matching algorithm more.
ratingWeight.example: Set higher (30%+) if you want to surface hidden gems with great reviews.

diversityWeight.title: Result Diversity
diversityWeight.description: Ensures variety in the final recommendation list.
diversityWeight.increase: Spreads recommendations across different genres, years, styles.
diversityWeight.decrease: May cluster similar items together if they all score well.
diversityWeight.example: High diversity prevents getting 10 Marvel movies in a row even if the user loves them.

settingsWatching.title: Shows You Watch
settingsWatching.subtitle: Let users track series they are watching, see upcoming episodes, and keep their list in sync with media server favorites (Emby/Jellyfin).
settingsWatching.loadFailed: Failed to load Shows You Watch configuration
settingsWatching.enableCaption: When disabled, the list and favorites sync are unavailable for all users.

settingsLegacyEmbeddings.title: Legacy Embedding Tables
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.deleteSuccess: Successfully deleted {{count}} rows from legacy tables
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.warningBody: Legacy embedding tables from before the multi-dimension migration were found. These tables are no longer used and can be safely deleted to free up storage.
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.tablesIntro: The following tables contain old embedding data:
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.dialogBody: This will permanently delete the following tables:
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.dialogFooter: This action cannot be undone. Your new dimension-specific embedding tables will not be affected.

settingsLibraryTitles.loadFailed: Failed to load library title configuration
settingsLibraryTitles.title: AI Recommendations Libraries
settingsLibraryTitles.subtitle: Configure naming templates and cover images for AI recommendation libraries. Users can override names in their personal settings.

settingsPage.personalPrefsSubtitle: Customize your AI recommendations library name as it appears in your media server.
settingsPage.personalPrefsFooter: Changes will apply the next time the "Update Permissions" job runs or when recommendations are regenerated. If you already have a library with the old name, you may need to manually delete it from your media server.
settingsPage.genreDiscoveryTabDisabled: Configure TMDb in Integrations first (API key).
settingsPage.genreDiscoveryRequiresTmdb: TMDb must be configured with a valid API key in the Integrations tab before you can edit genre-based Discovery strips.

language.defaultsSubtitle: Default interface and AI output languages for users who do not set their own overrides.
language.userSubtitle: Choose the interface language and the language for AI-generated taste synopses and recommendation explanations. Clear a field to use the server default.
language.effectiveAi: Effective AI language: {{lang}} — applies when taste profiles and recommendations are generated.

discovery.subtitle: AI-powered suggestions for content not in your library
discovery.disabledBody: Discovery is not enabled for your account. Contact your admin to enable missing content suggestions.
discovery.alertRequestEnabled: Click on any title to view on TMDb. Hover to request content via Seerr.
discovery.alertRequestDisabled: Content requests are not enabled for your account. You can browse suggestions but cannot request them.
discovery.emptyMovie: No movie suggestions yet. Click “Refresh” to generate personalized recommendations based on your watch history.
discovery.emptySeries: No series suggestions yet. Click “Refresh” to generate personalized recommendations based on your watch history.
discovery.expanding: Finding more content matching your filters…

discoveryGenreRows.sectionBlurb: Each row is a TMDb Discover strip. If a row lists several genres, TMDb returns titles that match all of them. Results exclude items already in your library, watched, or requested.
discoveryGenreRows.emptyStrip: No titles match this row after filtering.

discoveryStreaming.sectionBlurb: Popular titles and streaming momentum from JustWatch. Filter to titles not already in your library and request via Seerr when available.
discoveryStreaming.missingOnly: Only titles not in my library
discoveryStreaming.staleCache: Showing cached results; live data refresh failed.

seerrRequest.title: Request options
seerrRequest.subtitleMovie: Choose where and how “{{title}}” should be added in Radarr.
seerrRequest.subtitleSeries: Choose where and how “{{title}}” should be added in Radarr/Sonarr.
seerrRequest.noServersMovie: No Radarr servers returned from Seerr.
seerrRequest.noServersSeries: No Sonarr servers returned from Seerr.

graphExplorer.hintsDesktop: Click poster to explore • Click ⓘ for details • Drag to reposition • Scroll to zoom
graphExplorer.hintsMobile: Tap to explore • Long press for details

mediaGraph.searchPlaceholder: Search by mood, theme, or description…

seasonRequest.allUnavailable: All seasons are already available or have been requested.
seasonRequest.selectHint: Select the seasons you want to request.

globalSearch.emptyHint: Start typing to search your library
globalSearch.emptyHintDetail: Search by title, actor, director, genre, or keyword

assistant.title: Aperture Encore
assistant.subtitle: AI-powered recommendations
assistant.placeholder: Ask me anything about movies or shows…
assistant.welcomeBody: Your AI movie and TV recommendation assistant. Ask me to find something to watch, discover similar titles, or explore your personalized picks!
assistant.suggestion4: What sci-fi movies do you recommend?

welcomeModal.title: Welcome to Aperture
welcomeModal.subtitle: AI-Powered Movie Recommendations
welcomeModal.stepWelcomeP1: <0>Aperture</0> is your personal AI-powered movie recommendation engine that learns from your watch history to suggest films you'll love.
welcomeModal.stepWelcomeP2: It integrates with your Emby or Jellyfin media server to understand your tastes and deliver personalized recommendations directly to your home screen.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiP1: Aperture uses <0>AI embeddings</0> to understand movies at a deep level:
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi1: <0>Semantic Understanding:</0> Each movie is converted into a 1,536-dimensional vector that captures its themes, tone, genre, and style.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi2: <0>Taste Profile:</0> Your watch history is analyzed to build a unique "taste fingerprint" based on what you've enjoyed.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi3: <0>Vector Similarity:</0> We find movies whose embeddings are mathematically closest to your taste profile.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiP2: This means recommendations go beyond simple genre matching — a psychological thriller fan might get suggestions for tense dramas with similar themes, even if they're not labeled as "thrillers."
welcomeModal.stepScoringP1: Each recommended movie is scored using multiple factors:
welcomeModal.stepScoringTasteD: How closely the movie matches your overall taste profile
welcomeModal.stepScoringGenreD: Rewards movies that introduce you to new genres while staying relevant
welcomeModal.stepScoringCommunityD: Favors highly-rated films to ensure quality recommendations
welcomeModal.stepScoringDiversityD: Ensures variety so you don't get 50 superhero movies in a row
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP1: When viewing a movie's detail page, you'll see <0>"Similar Movies"</0> powered by the same AI technology.
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP2: These aren't just movies in the same genre — they're films with similar:
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP3: So if you loved "Inception," you might see mind-bending films that share its cerebral nature, not just other Christopher Nolan movies.
welcomeModal.stepDataP1: Aperture syncs with your media server to understand your preferences:
welcomeModal.stepDataLi1: <0>Watch History:</0> Movies you've watched, including play counts and recency
welcomeModal.stepDataLi2: <0>Favorites:</0> Movies you've marked as favorites get extra weight
welcomeModal.stepDataLi3: <0>Recency:</0> Recent watches influence your taste profile more than older ones
welcomeModal.stepDataP2: You can choose to include or exclude already-watched movies from your recommendations in your preferences.

explorationConfig.title: New Feature: Exploration AI
explorationConfig.subtitle: Configure a dedicated AI for the Explore page
explorationConfig.bodyP1: Aperture now supports a dedicated <0>Exploration AI provider</0> for the Explore page's semantic graph generation.
explorationConfig.feature1Body: Use a more powerful model to understand queries like "feel-good comedies" or "mind-bending sci-fi"
explorationConfig.feature2Body: AI analyzes your library to find meaningful thematic connections between items
explorationConfig.feature3Body: Use GPT-4 or Claude for exploration while keeping cheaper models for other functions

watchHistoryPage.emptySeries: No watch history found. Series you watch will appear here after the watch history sync runs.

watchHistoryPage.confirmSeriesBody: This will mark all episodes of "{{title}}" as unwatched in your media server and remove them from your Aperture watch history.

topPicksPage.emptySeries: No trending series yet. Top picks are calculated from watch history across all users. Make sure TV series watch history sync has run and multiple users have watched some episodes.

mediaServer.tagline: Discover your next favorite. Every time.
mediaServer.title: Connect Your Media Server
mediaServer.subtitle: Aperture integrates with your Emby or Jellyfin server to analyze viewing habits and generate personalized recommendations.
mediaServer.needApiKeyShort: Need an API key? In your media server dashboard, go to Settings → API Keys and create a new key for Aperture.
mediaServer.needApiKeyLong: Need an API key? In your media server dashboard, go to Settings → API Keys and create a new key for Aperture. This allows secure read-only access to your library metadata and watch history.
mediaServer.apiKeyHelper: Find this in your {{server}} dashboard under API Keys
mediaServer.passwordlessHelper: Enable this if your media server users don't have passwords set
mediaServer.securityWarningBody: Only enable this if your Aperture instance is on a private network with no internet exposure. If your server is accessible via reverse proxy or the internet, leave this disabled to prevent unauthorized access.

libraries.title: Select Libraries to Analyze
libraries.body: Choose which media libraries Aperture should include when generating recommendations. Only content from enabled libraries will be analyzed for viewing patterns and included in AI-powered suggestions. You can have multiple libraries of each type enabled.
libraries.whatNext: What happens next: Aperture will sync metadata from these libraries, analyze content similarities using AI embeddings, and track watch history to understand each user's preferences.

validate.title: Validate Setup
validate.subtitle: Checking that Aperture can access all required paths and your media server is reachable.
validate.allPassed: All checks passed! Your setup is ready to go.
validate.someFailed: Some checks failed. Please fix the issues above and retry, or check your docker-compose.yml volume mounts.

restore.title: Restore from Backup
restore.subtitle: If you have a previous Aperture backup, you can restore it here. This is useful when migrating to a new server or recovering from a failure.
restore.successRestored: Database restored successfully! The page will reload in 3 seconds to complete the restore process.
restore.startFreshBody: Set up Aperture from scratch. This is the recommended option for new installations.
restore.restoreCardBody: Restore all your data from a previous Aperture installation.
restore.dialogWarning: This will replace all current data with the backup. After restore, the setup wizard will reload with your restored data.
restore.dialogAboutToRestore: You are about to restore from:

users.tooltipLastAdmin: At least one admin must remain enabled
users.refreshTooltip: Refresh user list from media server
users.bodyParagraph: Choose which users should receive personalized AI recommendations. For each user, you can enable recommendations for Movies, TV Series, or both. Aperture analyzes each user's watch history individually to generate tailored suggestions that appear in their personal recommendation libraries.
users.noUsersWarning: No users found on your media server. Please ensure your media server has active user accounts, then click the refresh button to reload the list.
users.adminInfoCaption: Admin users have been automatically enabled to ensure you can log in and manage Aperture. At least one admin must stay enabled to maintain access to the admin dashboard.
users.otherUsersCaption: Enable recommendations for family members or other users on your media server. Each enabled user will get their own personalized recommendations based on their viewing history.
users.noteWarning: Users with both Movies and Series disabled will not receive any recommendations and won't be able to log in to Aperture. You can always change these settings later in Admin → Users.

complete.title: Setup Complete!
complete.subtitle: Aperture has created personalized recommendation libraries in your media server.
complete.issuesAlert: Some users were skipped or had issues. Users need watch history for recommendations to be generated. They will receive recommendations after watching more content and running the next sync.
complete.loginPrimary: Log in with your Media Server admin credentials
complete.loginSecondary: Use the same username and password you use for Emby/Jellyfin
complete.openServerPrimary: Open your media server to see the new libraries
complete.openServerSecondary: New 'AI Picks' libraries will appear for each enabled user in Aperture
complete.schedulesIntro: Aperture runs background jobs to keep recommendations fresh. Default schedules can be modified in Admin → Jobs.
complete.schedWatchHistoryDesc: Every 2 hours — Tracks what users are watching
complete.schedLibraryScanDesc: Daily at 2am — Syncs new movies and series from your media server
complete.schedEmbeddingsDesc: Daily at 3am — Processes content for AI recommendations
complete.schedAiRecsDesc: Weekly on Sunday at 4am — Generates personalized picks for each user
complete.schedTopPicksDesc: Daily at 5am — Updates global trending content libraries
complete.unlockIntro: After logging in, visit Admin → Settings to enable these additional features:
complete.featTraktDesc: Connect your Trakt account to import ratings, watchlists, and discover trending content
complete.featMetadataDesc: Enrich your library with additional metadata, ratings, and artwork from TMDb and OMDb
complete.featWatchingDesc: Automatically create a library of shows you're currently watching, perfect for catching up on your favorites, and ignoring other shows you're not interested in with new episodes.
complete.featUserPrefsDesc: Each user can customize their recommendation preferences and settings
complete.finishHint: Clicking Finish will disable the public setup wizard, secure Aperture, and redirect to login.

initialJobsStep.topPicksFailedDefault: Failed to create Top Picks libraries
initialJobsStep.title: Initialize Your Library
initialJobsStep.body: Aperture will now sync your media library, analyze content with AI, and generate personalized recommendations. This process runs through several steps and may take a few minutes depending on your library size.
initialJobsStep.bulletSyncMedia: Sync Media — Import movie and TV series metadata from your media server
initialJobsStep.bulletWatchHistory: Sync Watch History — Import viewing history to understand preferences
initialJobsStep.bulletEmbeddings: Generate Embeddings — Create AI vectors to understand content themes (uses OpenAI)
initialJobsStep.bulletRecs: Generate Recommendations — Create personalized picks for each user
initialJobsStep.bulletLibraries: Sync Libraries — Push recommendation libraries to your media server
initialJobsStep.headsUp: This can take anywhere from a few minutes to 30+ minutes depending on the size of your media libraries. Large collections with thousands of movies and episodes will take longer.
initialJobsStep.noLogsYet: No logs yet. Start initialization to see live output.

topPicksStep.title: Top Picks (Optional)
topPicksStep.body: Create global "what's popular" libraries based on aggregated watch history from all users. Unlike personalized AI recommendations, Top Picks show the same trending content to everyone - great for discovering what's popular on your server.
topPicksStep.enableSubtitle: Create trending content libraries visible to all users
topPicksStep.noteAdvanced: Advanced settings like popularity algorithm weights, time windows, and refresh schedules can be customized later in Admin → Settings → Top Picks.
topPicksStep.symlinkWarning: Symlinks require that Aperture can access your media files at the exact same paths that your media server uses. If paths differ between systems (e.g., Docker containers), use STRM files instead.

fileLocations.title: File Locations
fileLocations.body: Configure where your media server sees Aperture's library files. These paths must match how your media server accesses the volumes mounted in your Docker configuration.
fileLocations.autoDetectBody: Aperture can automatically detect the correct paths by comparing how your media server sees files with how Aperture sees them.
fileLocations.howToFindBody: Open any movie in your media server, go to Media Info, and look at the file path. If it shows /mnt/Movies/SomeMovie/file.mkv, your media path prefix is /mnt/.
fileLocations.apertureLibsHelp: Where does your media server see Aperture's output libraries?
fileLocations.helperApertureLibs: Path where your media server sees /aperture-libraries
fileLocations.mediaPrefixHelp: Where does your media server see your original media files?
fileLocations.symlinkHelper: Used for creating symlinks to your media files

aiRecs.title: Configure AI Recommendation Libraries
aiRecs.body: Aperture creates virtual libraries in your media server containing personalized recommendations for each user. Configure how these libraries are created and optionally customize their cover images.
aiRecs.strmVsSymlink: STRM vs Symlinks: STRM files work universally and support streaming URLs, while Symlinks provide direct file access (better for local playback) but require Aperture to access your media files at the same paths as your media server. For TV series, symlinks are recommended for better performance.
aiRecs.moviesSymlinkHelp: Creates symbolic links to original movie files
aiRecs.moviesStrmHelp: Creates .strm files with paths or streaming URLs
aiRecs.seriesSymlinkHelp: Creates symlinks to original season folders (recommended)

aiSetup.title: Configure AI / LLM Providers
aiSetup.cardEmbeddingsDesc: Required for semantic search and AI recommendations. Generates vector representations of your media library.
aiSetup.cardChatDesc: Powers the AI assistant for conversational interactions about your library.
aiSetup.cardTextGenDesc: Generates recommendation explanations, taste profiles, and summaries.
aiSetup.cardExplorationDesc: Powers the Explore page. A powerful model here enables conceptual searches like 'feel-good comedies'.
aiSetup.needKeyAfterLink: to create one. New accounts get $5 free credits. Typical usage costs ~$0.10-0.50/month.
aiSetup.bodyLead: Aperture uses AI to power its recommendation engine. Configure your AI providers below.
aiSetup.bodyTail: for the best experience, but you can explore other providers including local models.

topPicksAdmin.title: Top Picks
topPicksAdmin.subtitle: Create global "what's popular" libraries based on aggregated watch history from all users. Unlike personalized recommendations, Top Picks show the same content to everyone.
topPicksAdmin.loadFailed: Failed to load Top Picks configuration

emby_history.label: Local Watch History
emby_history.description: Based on what your users are watching

tmdb_trending_week.label: TMDB Trending (Week)
tmdb_trending_week.description: What's trending this week on TMDB

hybrid.label: Hybrid (Local + External)
hybrid.description: Blend local watch history with an external source

fields.timeWindowHelper: How far back to look at watch history

movies.settingsSubtitle: Configure data source, filters, and list size for movie Top Picks.
movies.listHelper: Select a MDBList to use for movie rankings
movies.largeListWarning: Large list — consider external source or increase minimum viewers to {{count}}
movies.languageFilterHint: Filter movies by original language. Leave empty to include all languages.
movies.listSizeHint: Limit to a specific number of top movies, or include all matches from your criteria.

series.settingsSubtitle: Configure data source, filters, and list size for series Top Picks.
series.listHelper: Select a MDBList to use for series rankings
series.largeListWarning: Large list — consider external source or increase minimum viewers to {{count}}
series.languageFilterHint: Filter series by original language. Leave empty to include all languages.
series.listSizeHint: Limit to a specific number of top series, or include all matches from your criteria.

output.title: Output Configuration
output.subtitle: Choose how Top Picks appear in your media server. You can enable multiple output types simultaneously.
output.accordionTitle: What's the difference between Library, Collection, and Playlist?
output.libraryBody: Appears as a separate library in your media server sidebar. Contains virtual copies (STRM files) of the media. Best for dedicated browsing of Top Picks.
output.collectionBody: Groups your existing library items into a "box set" that appears when browsing. Uses your original media files directly. Best for organization within existing libraries.
output.playlistBody: Creates an ordered playlist in the Playlists section. Uses your original media files directly. Best for sequential watching or shuffle play.
output.symlinkWarning: Symlinks require that Aperture can access your media files at the exact same paths that your media server uses. If paths differ between systems (e.g., Docker containers), use STRM files instead.

localAlgorithm.title: Local Popularity Algorithm
localAlgorithm.subtitle: Adjust the relative importance of each factor when using Local or Hybrid mode.
localAlgorithm.factors:
Unique Viewers = How many different people watched it (popularity breadth)
Play Count = Total number of plays across all users (engagement depth)
Completion Rate = How often people finish watching (quality signal)

autoRequest.title: Auto-Request Missing Content
autoRequest.subtitle: Automatically request missing Top Picks content through Seerr. Only works with external sources (TMDB, MDBList).
autoRequest.infoLine1: When enabled, Aperture will periodically check your Top Picks list for items not in your library and automatically submit requests to Seerr.
autoRequest.infoLine2: Make sure Seerr is configured in <1>Settings → Integrations</1>.
autoRequest.requiresExternal: Auto-request requires an external source (TMDB or MDBList)
autoRequest.moviesHelper: Limit how many movies to request per job run
autoRequest.seriesHelper: Limit how many series to request per job run

previewModal.emptyMatchedFiltered: No items match the selected language filter. {{total}} items available without filter.
previewModal.emptyMatched: No items found in your library matching this source.
previewModal.emptyMissingLocal: Local watch history source only shows items already in your library.
previewModal.emptyMissingFiltered: No missing items match the selected language filter. {{total}} items available without filter.
previewModal.emptyMissingAllInLibrary: All items from this source are already in your library!
previewModal.configureSeerr: Configure Seerr in <1>Settings → Integrations</1> to request missing items.
previewModal.seriesClickHint: Click on individual series to select seasons to request

posterRepair.title: Missing Poster Repair
posterRepair.description: Scan your Emby library for movies and series with missing poster images. This tool fetches replacement posters from TMDB and uploads them directly to Emby—no images are stored in Aperture.
posterRepair.noMissingFound: No items with missing posters found!
posterRepair.scanFailed: Failed to scan for missing posters
